scenario: Regime change operation with air campaign against fortified non-state actor using proxy forces

historicalCase.name: Second Lebanon War
historicalCase.summary: Israel attempted to destroy Hezbollah through air campaign and limited ground incursion. Hezbollah survived, Israel perceived as not achieving objectives.

strategicDynamics.factor: Air power insufficient against dug-in irregulars
strategicDynamics.description: Bunker networks and rocket dispersal limited damage
Leadership survived, command and control intact

strategicDynamics.factor: Ground invasion too limited and late
strategicDynamics.description: 33 days of air war before serious ground operations

outcome.geopoliticalShift: Hezbollah prestige enhanced; Israeli deterrence questioned; Iran encouraged

lessons.insight: Air campaigns cannot defeat dug-in irregular forces
caveats: Requires willing ground force or total annihilation

lessons.insight: Missile arsenaries create unacceptable costs for limited wars
Proxy forces can absorb punishment that states cannot
caveats: Requires external resupply and popular tolerance

matchingFactors: Strategic dynamic: Air power insufficient against dug-in irregulars
parallels.makaveliNote: Weak but instructive parallel. Consider Air power insufficient against dug-in irregulars with caution.
